_meta.source: They Had Names — theyhadnames.net/wills (will summary, compiler Stacy Ashmore Cole)
_meta.purpose: Rich extraction-target ground truth for the Georgia probate scraper. A multi-part document (will + codicil) — exercises document segmentation and per-document field extraction.
_meta.document_structure: One logical probate document spanning a will (signed 1861-11-19) and a codicil (signed 1861-12-07), both recorded together 1862-04-19. Across the FamilySearch collection this is multiple consecutive page-images.

children.name: John D. Barnard
children.note: his children are residuary beneficiaries via trust

other_kin_beneficiaries.name: John Barnard Chisolm
other_kin_beneficiaries.bequest: gold watch and chain, up to $150

other_kin_beneficiaries.name: Augusta Barnard Palmer
other_kin_beneficiaries.relation: daughter of Dr. B. M. Palmer of New Orleans
other_kin_beneficiaries.bequest: gold watch, chain and seal of similar cost

other_kin_beneficiaries.name: John Barnard Law
other_kin_beneficiaries.relation: son of the late Josiah Law
other_kin_beneficiaries.bequest: $500 in trust (held by Peter W. Fleming and Augustus Fleming) to aid his education

enslaved_persons.name: Becky
enslaved_persons.context: to be included in the lot to Nathaniel L. Barnard at division of the estate

enslaved_persons.group_description: Becky's seven children (and any future children)

enslaved_persons.name: Will
enslaved_persons.context: recommended to her kindest treatment and indulgence for his past fidelity

enslaved_persons.name: Peggy
enslaved_persons.bequeathed_to: Ann P. Barnard and Nathaniel Law Barnard
enslaved_persons.estate_interest_type: use of own time and profits of own labor

enslaved_persons.name: Mary E.
enslaved_persons.bequeathed_to: Ann P. Barnard and Nathaniel Law Barnard
enslaved_persons.estate_interest_type: use of own time and profits of own labor

enslaved_persons_unnamed_groups.description: negroes in Dougherty County in possession of Nathaniel L. Barnard, to be appraised and divided into two portions
negroes worked on the North Hampton plantation, allocated among wife and the trust for John D. Barnard's children

real_property.name: North Hampton plantation
real_property.instruction: not to be sold during the wife's natural life; sold at her death, proceeds divided per will item 5

real_property.description: lot and improvements in Laurel Grove Cemetery, Savannah
real_property.note: $2,000 of estate income appropriated to complete arrangements

monetary_bequests.conditions: annual maintenance allowance while estate kept together during/after the war
in trust for education; trustees Peter W. Fleming and Augustus Fleming

trust_instruments.beneficiaries: children of deceased son John D. Barnard
trust_instruments.terms: one-third of residue sold and reinvested, accumulated until John D. Barnard's children reach majority or marry; negroes to be sold in families and to kind and humane masters

estate_division.scheme: residue divided into three equal parts after the war

shares.interest: one-third, life estate; remainder to Nathaniel L. Barnard and children of John D. Barnard

shares.recipient: trust for children of John D. Barnard
shares.interest: one-third, held by executors as trustees

estate_division.hotchpot_note: division accounts for prior gifts to Nathaniel L. and John D. Barnard and property derived under Sarah M. Law's will and the wife's mother's will

executors.name: William Augustus Fleming
executors.relation: friend; son of Peter W. Fleming

court_jurisdiction: Liberty County Court of Ordinary, Georgia
